title: Bonney Sees Kuma’s Memories
description: On pages 2–4, this part focuses on Unknown person #1, York, Unknown person #2, Saturn, Bonney, King Bekori as Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.
scene: Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You'll only add yourself to the casualty list! Get out of there!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I got the idea for this effective policy from a country in the Far East!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They were able to curry favor with the celestial dragons by burning away all their trash, leaving a beautiful, spotless city....!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: One must have the heart to do the devil's work...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's the only way to ensure a nation becomes rich and beautiful.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The people are still resisting us, sire!

dialogue.speaker: York
dialogue.text: They're failing to see the greater good.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: kill four or five of them.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Anyone who disagrees with the King's Edict...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Is welcome to step forward now!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Stop, we'll execute you on the spot!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We can't have you get caught in the light!

dialogue.speaker: Bonney
dialogue.text: I've never seen Daddy so on edge...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There are consequences for those who take up arms against their king!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: This day would go down in history...

dialogue.speaker: King Bekori
dialogue.text: It seems sorbet has a new king.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: And to whom did the crown fall?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The people would not take no for an answer...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He became a king who lived in a beaten down, old church...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Bulldog-san is the one who's really running the country.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: king bulldog was our old, old king. His last reign wasn't half bad.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Sure we were poor, but our hearts were full-

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: MIND YOU, IT WOULDIVE BEEN EVEN NICER IF I COULD SAY THE SAME THING FOR MY WALLET!

summary: This part advances the Kuma and Bonney’s Tragedy through 54 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
